prompt:
Goal: Create a dramatic anime game-character promotional poster for Tachibana Uyuki-chan, inspired by a classic samurai fighting game character profile card.

Canvas: Wide 16:9 landscape poster, high-resolution, with a white, navy blue, black, and gold ink-brush aesthetic. The composition should feel like a Japanese sword-fighting game splash screen with layered calligraphy, paint splatters, and dynamic diagonal motion.

Main subject: A beautiful young female samurai occupies the right half of the image, shown from thighs up in a dynamic three-quarter pose. She has very long flowing deep navy blue hair whipping leftward across the poster, a white kimono top with loose sleeves, a dark blue hakama skirt, and a blue waist sash. She carries a sheathed katana at her waist with ornate gold fittings and blue wrapping. Her right hand is raised in a delicate gesture near the sword hilt, wearing a blue bead bracelet. Her face is intentionally obscured by a soft rectangular gray blur centered over the face, while the hair and outfit remain sharp.

Background and style: Use rough black and cobalt-blue brush strokes, icy blue watercolor splashes, gold paint streaks, scattered petals, and a textured paper background. The style is polished anime key art with crisp linework, glossy hair highlights, dramatic shadows, and elegant Japanese calligraphy. Add black ink borders and diagonal blue brush swashes near the bottom.

Top-left logo area: Add a metallic fantasy game logo reading “SAMURAI SPIRITS” with small Japanese katakana beneath it. Place it over a black-and-gold brush stroke.

Left profile area: Include a black brush label reading “秘剣 —— 燕返し”. Beneath it add two short Japanese flavor-text lines. Add a very large black calligraphy name “橘 右京ちゃん” across the lower-left to center area, with small romanized text “UYUKI TACHIBANNA” underneath. Include exactly 6 profile rows in a vertical list with black-gold label plaques and Japanese values: 1) “流派” / “自成我流”, 2) “誕生日” / “不明”, 3) “出身地” / “東海道”, 4) “武器名” / “小烏丸”, 5) “好きなもの” / “和菓子・甘味全般”, 6) “苦手なもの” / “不合理・不味いもの・虫”.

Dessert inset: Near the lower-left center, create a small framed food vignette on a dark lacquer tray. Include exactly 6 visible dessert elements: 1 strawberry parfait in a glass, 1 large strawberry, 1 green matcha sweet, 1 pale pink mochi, 1 darker pink mochi, and 1 skewer of dango. Add pink cherry blossoms beside the tray. Add a black brush caption reading “本日のご褒美” and yellow text “いちご大福”, followed by smaller white text “最高ですわ…!” with a small pink heart.

Right vertical quote: Along the far right, place vertical white Japanese text on a black brush strip: わたくしの信念に、甘味など不要——……ですが、嫌いでは、ありません。

Bottom slogan: Across the lower-right in large energetic Japanese brush lettering, write 甘味は、心の栄養——甘いもののためなら、もっと強くなれます！ mixing white and gold text, with the final phrase especially large and gold. Add blue-black brush strokes behind it for contrast.

Constraints: Preserve the exact wide poster layout, exactly 6 profile rows, exactly 6 dessert elements, no extra characters, no photorealism, no modern UI, no watermark, and keep all Japanese text legible where possible.
